Items.Title: 前端开发者之路第 1 天 | The Road to Frontend Developer Day 1
Items.Description:
正树钩

发表于你好美丽的生物！

开始这个“前端开发之路”的目的是让自己保持动力，学习和提高我的技术技能。 此外，我很乐意与与我同行的人分享我的一些奋斗、新发现或任何帮助我成为更好开发人员的技巧和窍门。

请允许我稍微解释一下我自己。 我的名字是 Masaki Hook，目前在华特迪士尼世界度假区（游艇和海滩/木板路地区）担任娱乐协调员。 我拥有中佛罗里达大学的计算机科学学士学位。 在过去的 6 个月里，我一直在自学 HTML、CSS、JavaScript、React、Tailwind/Bootstrap、SASS 和 Git 等前端/Web 开发技能，可以轻松地说我精通这些技术堆栈。 目前正在学习更多关于后端技能的知识，例如 GraphQL、MongoDB、Node.js/Express.js 等等。 是的，你猜对了！ 我是日本人，我确实喜欢动漫，但请注意，我必须看日文动漫，没有英文配音，否则你不是真正的动漫爱好者......开玩笑！ 我目前正在寻找入门级工作的工作，但近 2 个月来没有任何运气进入面试。 我目前正在努力弄清楚要学习或需要做什么。 然而，我并没有停下来提高自己。 老实说，我一直处于所谓的教程地狱中-最终开发者？ 有其他人加入或看到我一起学习和提高技能吗？ 我希望和我在同一条船上的其他人加入并一起学习！ 在这一点上，我将主要关注 React/JavaScript 技能以提高，但我还需要复习一些 HTML/CSS 或其他框架，例如 Bootstrap 或 Tailwind CSS，并学习更多的后端技术堆栈。

如果您是一位经验丰富的开发人员，并且有时间并且经历过困难并愿意分享您的一些技巧和窍门，或者只是不介意花费一些宝贵的时间来帮助您的新手开发人员，请随意随时发表评论并帮助我们，我非常感谢您投入时间和精力来提供帮助！

这是我的不和谐服务器： https ://discord.gg/6yZHJSh
您可以加入不和谐服务器并进行聊天！ 我也经常玩电子游戏，也许我可以流式传输我的学习内容或制作一些我很喜欢教的主题的教程视频，但请注意，我不是任何主题的专家，我只是非常了解某些主题！

明天，我将写这篇文章的实际技术方面，敬请期待！ 这是我旅程的开始！

非常感谢你阅读这篇文章，我希望你们都有一个神奇的一天！

快乐编码，

正树钩

一旦暂停，hookkl21 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，hookkl21 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，hookkl21 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 hookkl21 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 hookkl21 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 hookkl21 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Masaki Hook

Posted on Sep 6

Howdy beautiful creatures!

The purpose of starting this "Road to Frontend Developer" is to keep myself motivated, learn, and improve my technical skills. Also, I would love to share some of my struggles, new findings, or any tips and tricks that helped me be better developer to people who are on the same journey as I am.

Please allow me to explain a little bit about myself. My name is Masaki Hook, currently working as recreational coordinator at Walt Disney World Resort (Yacht and Beach/Boardwalk region). I have a bachelor's of Computer Science degree from University of Central Florida. For the past 6 months, I have been self studying Front-end/Web development skills such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Tailwind/Bootstrap, SASS, and Git to comfortably say I am proficient in these tech stacks. Currently studying on gaining more knowledge on Back-end Skills such as GraphQL, MongoDB, Node.js/Express.js to name few. And yes you guessed it! I am Japanese and I do love anime but please be warned, I do have to watch anime in Japanese, no English dub, or you are not true anime lover... Just kidding! XD

I am currently in the job hunting for entry level job, but haven't had any luck getting into the interview for almost 2 months now. I am currently struggling to figure out what to learn or need to do. However, I am not stopping to improve myself. I have been in so called tutorial hell if I am being honest :D and thought myself, there are people out there who is struggling as I am or worse, so why don't I make a blog posts about my journey to become a front-end developer? And have others join or see me learn and sharpen the skills together? I am hoping for others who are on the same boat as me to join and learn together! I will mainly focusing on React/JavaScript skills to improve at this point but I also need to brush up some of HTML/CSS or other frameworks such as Bootstrap or Tailwind CSS, and learn more back-end tech stacks.

If you are a very well experienced developer and who has time and have been through the struggles and willing to share some of your tips and tricks, or simply wouldn't mind spending some of your precious time helping your fellow newbie developer, please feel free to comment and gives us a hand whenever you can, and I really do appreciate putting your time and effort to help!

Here is my discord server: https://discord.gg/6yZHJSh
You can join the discord server and have chat! I also play video games a lot, and maybe I can stream my learning or making tutorial video for some topics that I am very comfortable teaching, but please be aware I am no expert on any topics, I just do know certain topics very well!

Tomorrow, I am going to write actual technical side of the post so stay tuned! This is the beginning of my journey!

Thank you very much for reading this posts, and I hope all of you have a magical day!

Happy Coding,

Masaki Hook
